data.author: nuclearsandwich
data.message:
Depend directly on python_cmake_module.

Since we use it directly, I think it ought to be added to the manifest.
As to why it's not strictly needed to build, I believe it's bubbling up
from an exported dependency chain starting with a
buildtool_export_depend in rosidl_generator_py.

data.author: ahuizxc
data.message: fixed using wrong type of stamp (#12)

data.author: gaoethan
data.message:
enable message_filters support of python interfaces and tests (#7)

* use ros2 subscriber with ros2 python client library

* replace ROS ROSTIME python interfaces with ROS2 ROSTIME

* use rclpy logging and log severity level

* enable directed.py test

* enable python test test_message_filters_cache.py
 - use new Subscriber
 - use ROS2 ROSTIME
 - enable test_all_funcs for cache interfaces
 - enable test_headerless to cache headerless message

* enable message filters python test test_approxsync.py
 - enable approximate policy test for messages with timestamp
 - enable approximate policy test for headerless messages
 - use ROS2 ROSTIME for time operations

* enable python interfaces and tests to build and add dependency

* fix the issue that zip object is not subscriptable

* fix doc and coding style issues

* remove redundant dependency to std_msgs

* optimize doc and context wrapping

* use semantic datatypes with Time and Duration

* use named logger instead of root logger

data.author: NikolausDemmel
data.message:
Fix segmentation fault on OS X 10.9 (clang / libc++)

Accessing front() of an empty std::deque is undefined behavior.
With gcc/libstdc++ it seems fine, on clang / libc++ it causes a segmentation fault.
